返回本批次

嘻哈先驱 Afrika Bambaataa 去世，享年 68 岁

Hip-hop pioneer, Afrika Bambaataa, dies aged 68

www.bbc.co.uk 作者 mellosouls 分数 57 评论 9 2026/04/10 12:17
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. TMZ 最先报道了他的死讯，称这位艺术家周四因癌症并发症在宾夕法尼亚州去世。 Bambaataa 出生于牙买加和巴巴多斯移民父母。青少年时期，他成为 Black Spades 帮派的成员，利用自己的领导能力组建了“Universal Zulu Nation”，旨在引导青年文化远离暴力，转向创造力。他 1982 年的热门歌曲《Planet Rock》为他赢得了全球认可，并被认为塑造了 20 世纪 80 年代的嘻哈音乐。他对嘻哈音乐的愿景将布朗克斯区变成了“嘻哈音乐的诞生地”。嘻哈联盟执行董事科蒂斯·布洛·沃克 (Kurtis Blow Walker) 牧师博士表示：“这种文化现在已遍及世界各个角落。”在 20 世纪 80 年代及以后，他继续广泛合作，与詹姆斯·布朗 (James Brown) 和约翰·莱登 (John Lydon) 等音乐家合作，并为具有政治意识的项目做出贡献，例如 1985 年的反种族隔离歌曲《太阳城》(Sun City)。

Original Summary

  1. TMZ, which was first to report his death, said the artist died in Pennsylvania due to complications from cancer on Thursday. Bambaataa was born to Jamaican and Barbadian immigrant parents.As a teen, he became a member of the Black Spades gang, parlaying his leadership abilities to form the Universal Zulu Nation, that sought to channel youth culture away from violence and towards creativity.His 1982 hit Planet Rock, won him global recognition and is credited with shaping hip-hop in the 1980s.His vision for hip-hop transformed the Bronx borough into "the birthplace of a culture that now reaches every corner of the world", said Reverend Dr Kurtis Blow Walker, the executive director of Hip Hop Alliance.Throughout the 1980s and beyond, he continued to collaborate widely, working with musicians including James Brown and John Lydon, and contributing to politically conscious projects such as the anti-apartheid song Sun City in 1985.

评论（默认中文）

contubernio 2026/04/10 13:46
Bambaataa 是一名连环性虐待者，说唱界的每个人在当时（90 年代初）都知道这一点，就像每个人都知道 R. Kelly（我在 92-94 年在广播电台运行过一个说唱节目）一样。
Bambaataa was a serial sexual abuser and everybody in the rap scene knew it back in the day (early 90s) same way everyone knew about R. Kelly (I ran a rap program on the radio in 92-94).

defrost 2026/04/10 13:10

在 HN 中查看
jimt1234 2026/04/10 13:14
Afrika Bambaataa 是我在 82 年左右爱上嘻哈音乐的一个主要原因。此外，我一直觉得“完美节拍”是一首比更流行的“行星摇滚”更好的歌曲。那时，“行星摇滚”是针对普通民谣的，“完美节拍”是针对摇滚乐的。普通人会在地板上跳舞，就像平常一样，然后，到了晚上，DJ 会播放“完美的节拍”，然后播放 - 具体来说，这部分： https://www.youtube.com/watch?t=229&v=rHQ11l4uiM4 。舞池会清空，否则你会被某个旋转的家伙抓住一只脚。美好时光。

我仍在努力消化以后生活中针对巴姆的所有性虐待指控。

安息吧巴姆

Afrika Bambaataa is a major reason I fell in love with hip hop back around '82. Further, I've always felt "perfect beat" is a much better song than the more popular "planet rock". Back then, "planet rock" was for regular folk, "perfect beat" was for the breakers. Regular folk would be dancing on the floor, just like normal, and then, later in the evening, the DJ would drop "perfect beat" and it was on - specifically, this part: https://www.youtube.com/watch?t=229&v=rHQ11l4uiM4 . The dance floor would clear, otherwise you'd catch a foot from some dude spinning around. Good times.

I'm still trying to digest all the s3xual abuse allegations against Bam later in life.

RIP Bam

在 HN 中查看
wahnfrieden 2026/04/10 13:24
这些指控在他晚年才被公开，但事件可以追溯到 80 年代和 90 年代，也就是他的整个职业生涯。确实在小便中休息
The allegations became public later in his life but the incidents go back to the 80s and 90s, his whole career. Rest In Piss indeed
在 HN 中查看
Teever 2026/04/10 13:14
19 岁时，我在当地一家现场音乐场所从事清理桌子和搬运酒的工作，断断续续地工作了七年多。

尽管我很喜欢现场音乐，但过了一段时间，它就成了一份工作，但时不时会有一位令人难以置信的音乐家出现，直到我上班时我才知道，我问我的同事那天晚上谁在演奏。

一天晚上，我一进门，发现是他妈的 DJ Africa Bambaataa，我下巴都惊掉了！ 现在我不太喜欢嘻哈音乐，但我听过他的几张专辑，我知道他的音乐是非凡的，我很震惊这样一个传奇人物在我的小镇上演奏。

最疯狂的是，在大约 800 人的容量中，只有大约 100 人出现，但这些人中的每一个都可以 舞蹈。

场地有一个连接到天花板的旧音响室，可以通过摇摇晃晃的旧螺旋楼梯进入，因为那天晚上楼梯太慢了，我大部分时间都在那里沉浸在这种体验中。

我一生中看过很多现场表演，但那一场最引人注目。

I fell into a job bussing tables and porting alcohol at a local live music venue when I was 19 and I worked there off and on over seven years.

As much as I love live music after a while it just sort of became a job, but every now and again an incredible musician would come through and I wouldn’t know until I showed up for my shift and I asked my coworkers who was playing that night.

One night I come in and my jaw drops when find out it’s fucking DJ Africa Bambaataa! Now I’m not big into hip hop but I had listened to a few of his albums and I knew his music was phenomenal and I was shocked such a legend was playing in my town.

The crazy part is only like 100 people showed up out of a capacity of like 800 but every single one of those people could dance.

The venue had an old sound booth that was attached to ceiling and was accessible with a rickety old spiral staircase, as it was so slow that night I spent most of my time up there just soaking in that experience.

I’ve seen a lot of live shows in my day but that one stands out.

为什么这与 HN 相关？
Why is this relevant to HN?
在 HN 中查看
mind-blight 2026/04/10 13:05
因为它是非洲班巴塔。他发明了全新的音乐制作技术——这已经足以让他在这里发挥作用——影响了我们许多人每天听的音乐。
Because it's Afrika Bambaataa. He invented entirely new techniques for making music - which is already enough for him to be relevant here - that influences what many of us listen to daily.

jimt1234 2026/04/10 13:26
早在 80 年代初，在了解了采样音乐后，大多数人的下一个问题是：“Kraftwerk 到底是谁？”哈哈
Back in the early-80s, after learning about sampled music, most people's next question was, "Who the fuck is Kraftwerk?" LOL
